changes: Reordered struct fields: moved `exit_code` and `paused_at` earlier in Meta struct initialization
Applied multi-line formatting to 4 code blocks for clippy compliance
Updated 8 test struct initializations to match reordered fields
file_insights.impact: Structural and stylistic changes; all tests pass

changes: Reformatted 2 multi-line assignments for code style compliance
file_insights.impact: Stylistic changes only; no logic changes

patterns_discovered.description: Commit appears to be result of `cargo fmt` + `cargo clippy` execution, addressing both code style and subtle optimizations
All 8 test struct initializations updated in lockstep with production code field reordering
Iterator pattern `filter().last()` replaced with direct `rfind()` for efficiency

gotchas_discovered.description: `exit_code` and `paused_at` fields reordered in Meta struct; unclear if driven by derive macro, serde, or style convention. Failure to update all struct initializations (test and production) would cause compilation errors.
8 separate test struct initializations must be manually maintained in sync. Future Meta field changes will require updates across multiple test locations, increasing maintenance burden.
Multi-line reformatting suggests strict line-length enforcement (likely 100 chars); violations would block `make check`. Developers must be aware of this constraint.

approach_outcome.description: All tests pass after field reordering and formatting pass. Commit represents successful execution of `make check` (test + clippy + fmt).
approach_outcome.validation: Production code compiles, all test fixtures match new field order, no logic regressions

recommendations.suggestion: Implement Meta struct builder pattern or use `Default` trait to reduce test boilerplate across 8+ test initializations
Add `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` to Meta struct to catch field reordering/missing field issues at serialization time
Audit other uses of `lines().filter().last()` pattern; consider systematic replacement with `rfind()` for consistency
Consider centralizing test Meta construction in a helper factory function to eliminate duplication and reduce future maintenance
